summary: Iter 37 — full e2e test of FOUR changes in one iteration: (1) RunEvalModal multi-view selection, (2) Datasets cross-page selection + Save/Update view, (3) backend duplicate-name 409 protection, (4) Step 1/2/3 modal cleanups. Backend pytest 16/16 PASS (12 existing + 4 new duplicate-name tests). RunEvalModal multi-view union works end-to-end: 2 chips, 4 union items, single-batch POST submitted 4 jobs. Step 2 'Runs' label and editable input verified. Step 3 Submit button correctly reads just 'Submit', review-jobs-count gone, review-total-jobs='4'. CRITICAL FINDING: cross-page selection persistence is BROKEN — DatasetsPage.js useEffect at lines 201-203 wipes selectedKeys whenever `page` changes, defeating the whole cross-page feature.

integration_issues.flow: Datasets cross-page selection persistence (view load + paginate)
integration_issues.issue: DatasetsPage.js:201-203 has `useEffect(() => { setSelectedKeys(new Set()); }, [typeFilter, page])`. The `page` dep wipes the entire selection on every pagination action. Verified in UI: loaded 'smoke view A' (2 items live on dataset page 2). pickView calls setSelectedKeys(nextKeys) — but the items are not on the visible page 1 (0 checked). Paginating Next → 0 checked. Paginating back to page 1 → 0 checked (selection wiped). The `selectionItemsForView` memo therefore reads selectedKeys.size=0, so Save-as-new-view / Update-view will silently miss the off-page items that pickView seeded.

action_items: DatasetsPage.js:201-203 — REMOVE `page` from useEffect dep array (keep `typeFilter` only). page-pagination must NOT wipe selectedKeys. Type-filter change is an intentional corpus narrowing so clearing there is fine. After the fix: (a) pickView's pre-selection survives navigation, (b) Save-as-new-view payload spans all pages, (c) Update view diff payload spans all pages, (d) checkbox state on revisiting page 1 still shows the originally-ticked rows.
critical_code_review_comments: RunEvalModal.applyView correctly dedupes union by problem.name (line 538); missing-items toast text is good. activeViews chip strip + clear-btn-<view_id> + clear-all all wired with correct testids.

Backend create_dataset_view + update_dataset_view duplicate-name regex uses `re.escape` correctly (server.py:1768, 1819). PATCH excludes self via `{view_id: {$ne: view_id}}` so self-rename does not 409. All four duplicate-name pytest tests pass (including whitespace + case-insensitive variants).

DatasetsPage selectionItemsForView (line 331) parses composite keys from selectedKeys Set — semantically correct for cross-page, BUT the useEffect bug at line 201 nullifies this whenever the user paginates.
Submit button (RunEvalModal:1540) reads just 'Submit' — confirmed. review-jobs-count testid removed — confirmed.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (multi-view + step1/2/3 cleanups + duplicate-name + chip strip all pass; cross-page selection broken)

seed_data_creation: Pre-existing seeds 'smoke view A' (f259db17...) and 'smoke view B' (5ea36aaa...) retained intact. One transient eval batch 'TEST_pw_multiview_<ts>' (4 jobs, agent=full_stack_app_builder_cloud_v8_sonnet_4_5) was submitted during the multi-view union test — left in place because it proves the union submission landed in MongoDB as a single 4-job batch (visible in /evals page first row). No dataset_views rows were created or deleted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite: /app/backend/tests/test_dataset_views.py now has 16 tests (~2.6s) covering CRUD + the new duplicate-name 409 contract. Seed views 'smoke view A' (f259db1712f44c65b3966bebde05e6d8, 2 items) and 'smoke view B' (5ea36aaaa8744afc93d41be32afac046, 2 items) are still in MongoDB. Frontend testids verified live: eval-active-views-chips, eval-active-view-chip-<id>, eval-active-view-clear-btn-<id>, eval-active-views-clear-all, selected-problem-<name>, review-total-jobs (review-jobs-count removed), submit-eval-button, datasets-update-view-btn, datasets-save-view-btn. After main agent fixes the page-dep useEffect bug, re-run a quick UI test: load smoke view A, paginate next, paginate back, assert at least 1 checkbox stays checked on page 1.
rca of the issue: Cross-page selection bug RCA — In DatasetsPage.js the useEffect `useEffect(() => { setSelectedKeys(new Set()); }, [typeFilter, page])` (lines 201-203) was retained from before the 'cross-page selection' feature was added. The intent of that effect was to scrub stale ids when the user changes the dataset_type filter (because typeFilter change refetches a different corpus where the old keys no longer apply). However, it ALSO scrubs on every page change, which directly contradicts the new cross-page selection requirement. Reproduction: (1) load /datasets, (2) click Views → smoke view A, (3) observe 0 checked on visible page (items live on page 2), (4) click Next page button — selection wiped, 0 checked on page 2 as well. Mitigation: remove `page` from the dep array (one-line change): `useEffect(() => { setSelectedKeys(new Set()); }, [typeFilter])`. Optionally also keep a guard so the effect doesn't run during pickView's initial commit (pickView sets typeFilter='all' + page=0 + selectedKeys=nextKeys back-to-back — if typeFilter wasn't already 'all', the effect would fire and overwrite nextKeys; if it was already 'all', React's bail-out skips it — safer to use a ref-based 'first time after pickView' guard or just rely on render ordering since setSelectedKeys is called after setTypeFilter).
